summary:
The early stages of neurocysticercosis are marked by active inflammation and associated perilesional edema due to an ongoing immune response against a living or degenerating cyst. In contrast, during the mature calcified stage—the last phase—the cyst lacks viability and any residual inflammation; therefore, no edema exists at that point. This makes it clear why only the calcified form shows complete resolution of symptoms related to swelling from infection-related reactions within brain tissue.

Final answer:

\boxed{\text{Calcified Stage}}
